metadata.creator: Walt Disney Records and Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc.
metadata.description:
I know song 7 on this soundtrack receives flack from true Phil Collins fans but I got this album from Discogs to preserve lost recordings. 
I think the reason why a new backing track was made for song 7 is because they wanted the end title to end in the same key as the movie version.
Either that or because the dubbing studio in Japan didn't have access to the backing track for Phil's original recording. 

But if you want song 7 to be somewhat the same as Phil's original recording, you can download the song and put it into a website that transposes recordings.
Once you have it in the website, transpose it up a half step so the opening key is Gb/F# instead of F. 
Alternatively, you can wait for me to upload what I call version 2.0
Version 2.0 of this soundtrack includes two additional songs and an improved version of song 7 which I will reveal when I upload that.

Regardless, we have reused recordings from the English soundtrack.
Songs 4 and 9-12 are reused from the English soundtrack and thankfully, the yell was left in for song 13.
Here's the track list for the TARZAN Soundtrack in Japanese.

Two Worlds - Marsa Sakamoto
You’ll Be in My Heart - Fujita Toshiko & Marsa Sakamoto
Son of Man - Marsa Sakamoto
Trashin’ the Camp - Rosie O’Donnell, Phil Collins & Cast - TARZAN
Strangers Like Me - Marsa Sakamoto
Two Worlds (Reprise) - Marsa Sakamoto
You’ll Be in My Heart (End Title) - Marsa Sakamoto
Two Worlds (End Title) - Marsa Sakamoto
A Wondrous Place (Score) - Mark Mancina & Phil Collins
Moves Like an Ape, Looks Like a Man (Score) - Mark Mancina & Phil Collins
The Gorillas (Score) - Mark Mancina & Phil Collins
One Family (Score) - Mark Mancina & Phil Collins
Two Worlds (Finale) - Marsa Sakamoto
metadata.subject: TARZAN
metadata.title: TARZAN ターザン (An Original Walt Disney Records Soundtrack)
metadata.subject: Soundtrack
Japanese
Phil Collins
Marsa Sakamoto
